html:
[4045]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旅泊 - Compound meaning 'to travel and lodge,' often used to describe a wandering or temporary stay.
  • 顧降 - Means 'to visit' or 'to descend upon,' implying a respectful or noble visit.
  • 凡陋之士 - A modest self-reference meaning 'a common and humble person.'
  • 枕席 - Refers to marital relations or intimate encounters.
  • 絶色 - Means 'beyond compare' or 'exquisite beauty.'
  • 豪贵之女 - Refers to a wealthy and noblewoman.
  • 酒色干懐 - Means 'to be tempted by wine and women.'
  • 恩意欵洽 - Describes a deep and affectionate relationship.
  • 生祸 - Means 'to cause trouble' or 'to bring disaster.'
  • 絶矣 - Means 'completely stopped' or 'utterly finished.'
  • 集异记 - A collection of strange stories and anecdotes.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邬涛 - A historical figure or fictional character, whose name indicates he is from the region of Wú (Wu).
  • 汝南 - A historical region in present-day Henan Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 婺州义乌县 - A specific location in Zhejiang Province, known for its historical and cultural importance.
  • 坟典 - Refers to the Five Classics and other important ancient texts.
  • 道术 - Refers to Daoist practices or techniques.
  • 王氏小娘子 - Refers to a young lady from the Wang family, indicating her noble background.
  • 银烛 - Refers to a candle made of silver, symbolizing luxury and wealth.
  • 酒食 - Refers to food and drink, often used in social and ceremonial contexts.
  • 符 - Refers to talismans or magical symbols used in Daoist rituals.
  • 呪水 - Refers to water blessed with a spell or incantation, used for purification or protection.
  • 杨景霄 - A historical figure or fictional character, likely a Daoist master or scholar.
  • 断之 - Means 'to break the spell' or 'to exorcise.'
  • 怨恨 - Means 'resentment' or 'anger.'
  • 絶矣 - Means 'completely stopped' or 'utterly finished.'

Proper Nouns

  • 邬涛 - A historical figure or fictional character.
  • 汝南 - A historical region.
  • 婺州义乌县 - A specific location.
  • 坟典 - A collective term for important ancient texts.
  • 道术 - A term for Daoist practices.
  • 王氏小娘子 - A character name.
  • 银烛 - A term for a silver candle.
  • 酒食 - A term for food and drink.
  • 符 - A term for talismans or magical symbols.
  • 呪水 - A term for water blessed with a spell.
  • 杨景霄 - A historical figure or fictional character.
  • 集异记 - A collection of strange stories.

[4046]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大和四年春 - Refers to the spring of the fourth year of the Dahe era, a historical dating system.
  • 監州防御使 - Refers to the title of a military governor in charge of a region, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 曾孝安有孫曰季衡 - Introduces the main character, Zeng Jiheng, as the grandson of Zeng Xiao'an.
  • 居使宅西偏院 - Describes Zeng Jiheng's residence, specifically the western wing of the governor's mansion.
  • 室屋壯麗 - Means 'magnificent and splendid,' describing the luxurious nature of the room.
  • 獨處之 - Means 'lived alone there,' indicating Zeng Jiheng's solitary living situation.
  • 晝日其魂或見於此 - Suggests that the ghost of a deceased woman might be seen during the day in this room.
  • 頻注名香 - Means 'frequently burned incense,' suggesting a ritual or attempt to attract the spirit.
  • 步遊閑處 - Means 'strolled in quiet places,' describing his leisurely walks.
  • 恍然凝思 - Means 'suddenly deep in thought,' indicating a moment of intense reflection.
  • 雙鬟前揖 - Describes a servant girl with two buns in her hair who bows before him, a common depiction of a female servant in classical literature.
  • 王家小娘子遣某來傳達厚意 - Means 'Miss Wang has sent me to convey her deep respect,' indicating a message from the deceased woman.
  • 欲面拜郎君 - Means 'wants to pay respects to you personally,' showing the ghost's intention to meet Zeng Jiheng.
  • 瞥然而沒 - Means 'disappeared suddenly,' describing the swift disappearance of the servant girl.
  • 異香襲衣 - Means 'a strange fragrance filled the air,' indicating the presence of a supernatural scent.
  • 束帶伺之 - Means 'dressed up and waited for her,' showing Zeng Jiheng's preparation to meet the ghost.
  • 神仙中人也 - Means 'a person of divine beauty,' describing the ghost's appearance.
  • 問其姓氏 - Means 'asked about her name and surname,' indicating Zeng Jiheng's curiosity about the ghost's identity.
  • 某姓王字麗真 - Means 'I am Wang Lizi,' providing the ghost's name.
  • 父今為重鎮 - Means 'my father is now a high-ranking official,' indicating the ghost's family status.
  • 侍從大人牧此城 - Means 'I served as a companion to a nobleman who governed this city,' providing background on the ghost's past.
  • 據此室無何物故 - Means 'I died in this room without any possessions,' explaining the ghost's death.
  • 感君思深 - Means 'I deeply appreciate your thoughts,' indicating the ghost's gratitude towards Zeng Jiheng.
  • 幽壤所以不間存沒 - Means 'the underworld does not distinguish between the living and the dead,' suggesting the ghost's ability to interact with the living.
  • 頗思神防 - Means 'I often think of you,' showing the ghost's affection for Zeng Jiheng.
  • 其來 - Means 'her coming,' indicating the ghost's visit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大和四年 - Refers to the fourth year of the Dahe era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 監州防御使 - A title for a military governor, indicating a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 王家小娘子 - Refers to a young woman from the Wang family, a noble family in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 神仙中人 - A term used to describe someone of extraordinary beauty, often associated with deities or immortals in Chinese mythology.
  • 幽壤 - Refers to the underworld, a concept in Chinese folklore where the spirits of the dead reside.

Proper Nouns

  • 曾孝安 - The name of Zeng Xiao'an, Zeng Jiheng's grandfather.
  • 曾季衡 - The name of Zeng Jiheng, the main character.

[4047]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乆矣但非吉日良时 - For a long time, it was not a good day or auspicious time.
  • 今方契愿 - Now it is in accordance with our wishes.
  • 幸垂留意 - I hope you will pay attention.
  • 季衡留之欵防 - Ji Heng stayed and invited him to stay.
  • 翌日此時再防 - Tomorrow at this time, meet again.
  • 慎勿泄于人 - Be careful not to tell anyone.
  • 是日女郎一见季衡容色惨怛语声嘶咽 - On that day, the young lady saw Ji Heng and her expression was sad, her voice was choked with sobs.
  • 何为约而泄于人 - Why did you break your promise and tell others?
  • 殆非君之过亦防数尽耳 - It is not entirely your fault; perhaps your number of days is up.
  • 五原分袂真吴越燕拆莺离芳草竭年少烟花处处春北邙空恨清秋月 - Poem with metaphorical and symbolic language, e.g., '五原分袂' (parting at the five origins) and '燕拆莺离' (swallows and orioles parting) for separation, '芳草竭' (the fragrant grass exhausted) and '烟花处处春' (flowers and fireworks everywhere in spring) for beauty and transience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 季衡 - A name, likely referring to a character in the story.
  • 大父麾下将校 - Under the command of the father-in-law's generals, indicating a military context.
  • 艳丽 - Refers to a beautiful woman, possibly a title or nickname.
  • 北邙 - A hill near Luoyang, often associated with tombs and the afterlife in Chinese literature.
  • 清秋月 - Clear autumn moon, a common poetic image symbolizing melancholy and reflection.

Proper Nouns

  • 季衡 - A character in the story.
  • 艳丽 - A character or title, likely a beautiful woman.
  • 北邙 - A hill near Luoyang, often associated with tombs and the afterlife in Chinese literature.
  • 清秋月 - A poetic image symbolizing melancholy and reflection.

[4048]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 篇曰 - A phrase indicating the start of a poem or song, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 莎草青青鴈欲归 - A poetic description of a scene where reeds are lush and geese are about to return, suggesting a sense of longing or nostalgia.
  • 玉腮珠泪洒临歧 - Describes a woman with tears of pearl-like quality streaming down her face as she bids farewell, emphasizing emotional intensity.
  • 云鬟飘去香风尽 - Implies the departure of a woman with her hair like clouds and the scent of her perfume fading, symbolizing a poignant parting.
  • 愁见莺啼红枝 - Expresses sorrow at hearing the yellow oriole sing on a red branch, possibly alluding to the beauty of spring that contrasts with the sadness of separation.
  • 襦带解蹙金结花合子 - Refers to the act of untying a silk sash and tying it into a flower-like knot, likely a gesture of affection or farewell.
  • 翠玉双鳯翘 - Describes a pair of jade phoenix ornaments, often used as hairpins or decorative items, symbolizing elegance and grace.
  • 小金缕花如意 - Refers to a small golden如意 (a traditional Chinese good luck charm), possibly used as a gift.
  • 非一甲子无相见期 - Indicates that they will not meet again for sixty years, a significant period in Chinese culture.
  • 北邙空恨清秋月也 - Suggests a lingering regret or sorrow over the clear autumn moon in the North邙 (a place associated with cemeteries and the afterlife).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 莎草 - Refers to reeds or grass, often used in classical poetry to evoke a sense of nature and tranquility.
  • 鴈 - Geese, which are often associated with migration and the passage of time in Chinese literature.
  • 玉腮 - Describes a woman's face as smooth and beautiful as jade, a common metaphor in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 珠泪 - Pearls are often used as a metaphor for tears, emphasizing their value and the depth of emotion.
  • 临歧 - A term used to describe a moment of parting or a crossroads, often imbued with a sense of uncertainty or sadness.
  • 云鬟 - Refers to a woman's hair styled in a cloud-like shape, often associated with beauty and elegance.
  • 翠玉双鳯翘 - Phoenixes are mythical birds in Chinese culture, symbolizing immortality and good fortune. Jade is a precious material, often associated with purity and nobility.
  • 小金缕花如意 - The如意 (auspicious object) is a traditional Chinese good luck charm, often given as a gift to wish for happiness and prosperity.
  • 北邙 - A mountain range near Luoyang, historically known for its cemeteries and associated with the afterlife.

Proper Nouns

  • 季衡 - A proper name, likely referring to a male character in the story.
  • 王回 - Another proper name, possibly a mentor or friend of the main character.
  • 五原纫针妇人 - Refers to a woman from the Five Originals who is skilled in needlework, possibly a historical or fictional figure.
  • 王使君之爱女 - Refers to the daughter of a high-ranking official, adding a layer of social status and tragedy to the story.

[4049]

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 进士 - A degree obtained by passing the imperial examination, indicating a high level of education and potential for government service.
  • 大和 - A reign title of Emperor Wenzong of Tang (827-840).
  • 五原路 - A specific route or region in the Tang Dynasty, possibly referring to a military or trade route.
  • 党羌 - Refers to the Dang and Qiang tribes, ethnic groups in the northwest of China.
  • 奉天 - A place name, possibly referring to Fengtian (modern-day Shaanxi).
  • 洛源镇帅 - The military governor of Luoyuan, a position of significant power and responsibility.
  • 李文恱 - Li Wenye, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, known for his integrity and service.
  • 元和十三年 - The year 818 during the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang.

Proper Nouns

  • 赵合 - Zhao He, the main character of the story.
  • 李氏 - The woman's surname.
  • 奉天 - A place name, possibly referring to Fengtian (modern-day Shaanxi).
  • 洛源镇帅 - The military governor of Luoyuan.
  • 李文恱 - Li Wenye, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 元和十三年 - The year 818 during the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang.

[4050]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 守五原 - Defending Wuyuan; Wuyuan is a place name, likely referring to a strategic location.
  • 犬戎 - Xianrong; an ancient ethnic group often mentioned in Chinese historical texts.
  • 圍逼城池之四隅 - Surrounding and attacking the four corners of the city walls.
  • 兵各厚十数里 - Each side of the army was several miles thick.
  • 連弩洒雨 - Crossbows firing arrows like rain.
  • 飛梯排云 - Ladders reaching into the clouds; metaphorically, very tall ladders.
  • 穿壁決濠 - Boring through walls and cutting through moats.
  • 城中戶而汲者矢如猬毛 - Those drawing water from the well in the city were hit by arrows as dense as a hedgehog's spines.
  • 當其時御捍之兵才三千 - At that time, the defending troops numbered only three thousand.
  • 激厲其居人婦女老幼土而立者不知寒餒 - Encouraging the residents, women, and children to stand in the well and draw water, they were unaware of hunger and cold.
  • 獨腳樓 - One-legged tower; a siege structure.
  • 巨細咸得窺之 - Both large and small could see it.
  • 設竒計定中其樓立碎 - Devised a clever plan and hit the tower, causing it to collapse immediately.
  • 酋愕然以為神功 - The chieftain was astonished and believed it to be a divine feat.
  • 慎勿拆屋燒吾且為汝取薪積於城下 - Do not tear down houses or burn them; I will bring wood and pile it at the base of the wall.
  • 許人釣上 - Allow people to fish up; metaphorically, to retrieve objects.
  • 城之四隅多有人物行動聲 - There were many sounds of people and objects moving in the four corners of the city.
  • 言云夜攻城耳 - They said they would attack the city at night.
  • 城中懾栗不敢暫安 - The city was terrified and could not rest for a moment.
  • 不然潛以鐡索下燭而照之乃空驅牛羊行 - It was not so; we secretly lowered an iron chain with a torch and saw that they were merely driving cattle and sheep.
  • 西北隅被攻摧十余丈將入遇昏晦 - The northwest corner was attacked and breached for more than ten feet, ready to enter when it became dark and overcast.
  • 群胡大喜縱酒狂歌云候明晨而入 - The Xianrong tribesmen were very happy, drinking and singing wildly, saying they would enter at dawn.
  • 以馬弩五百張而擬之 - We prepared five hundred horse crossbows against them.
  • 遂下皮牆障之一夕並工暗築不使 - We then lowered the leather wall defenses and worked all night to secretly repair them without letting them know.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 五原 - A strategic location, likely referring to a specific region or city in ancient China.
  • 犬戎 - An ancient ethnic group, often mentioned in historical texts as adversaries of the Chinese states.
  • 圍逼城池之四隅 - A common military tactic in ancient warfare, where attackers would surround and attack the weakest points of a city's defenses.
  • 連弩洒雨飛梯排云穿壁決濠 - Descriptions of various siege tactics and weapons used in ancient warfare.
  • 獨腳樓 - A type of siege tower, often used to breach city walls.
  • 設竒計定中其樓立碎 - Refers to the use of clever military strategies to counteract enemy tactics.
  • 慎勿拆屋燒吾且為汝取薪積於城下 - A strategic move to prevent the enemy from using the city's resources against its defenders.
  • 許人釣上 - Metaphorically, allowing people to retrieve objects or information from a dangerous situation.
  • 城之四隅多有人物行動聲 - Sounds of movement can be used to create psychological pressure on the defenders.
  • 言云夜攻城耳 - A common tactic to create fear and uncertainty among the defenders.
  • 城中懾栗不敢暫安 - The psychological impact of constant attacks on the morale of the defenders.
  • 不然潛以鐡索下燭而照之乃空驅牛羊行 - A clever use of surveillance and deception to counteract enemy movements.
  • 西北隅被攻摧十余丈將入遇昏晦 - Describes a critical moment in the siege where the defenders are on the brink of defeat.
  • 群胡大喜縱酒狂歌云候明晨而入 - The enemy's confidence and celebration after a successful breach.
  • 以馬弩五百張而擬之 - Preparation of a large number of crossbows to counteract the enemy's advantage.
  • 遂下皮牆障之一夕並工暗築不使 - Secretly repairing defenses to regain the upper hand.

Proper Nouns

  • 五原 - A strategic location, likely referring to a specific region or city in ancient China.
  • 犬戎 - An ancient ethnic group, often mentioned in historical texts as adversaries of the Chinese states.

[4051]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有声涤之以水时寒来日氷坚城之莹如银不可攻击 - Complex sentence with archaic syntax; 'there was a sound as water was used to wash [something]' followed by description of the city being cold, ice-covered, and gleaming like silver, making it impregnable.
  • 穿壁而夺之如飞 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'pierced the wall and took it away as if flying' — likely referring to a daring theft or attack.
  • 钓其长幼妇女百余人得其尽归 - '钓' here is metaphorical, not literal fishing; may mean 'to ransom' or 'to lure back' the women and children.
  • 时邠泾救兵二万人临其境股栗不进 - '股栗' is a classical idiom meaning 'trembling at the knees' — a sign of fear or hesitation.
  • 以此利害则余之功及斯城不细 - '利害' refers to the consequences or outcomes; '不细' is an idiom meaning 'not small' or 'not insignificant.'
  • 余闻钟陵韦夫人旧筑一隄将防水潦 - '旧筑一隄' refers to a dike built in the past; '防水潦' is a classical expression for 'preventing floods.'
  • 周公感其功而奏立德政碑峩然 - '奏立' is an official act of establishing something; '德政碑' is a 'monument of virtue and good governance,' and '峩然' is an adjectival term meaning 'majestic.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 羌酋 - Refers to the Qiang people, a group of ethnic minorities in ancient China, often associated with warfare and conflict with Central Chinese states.
  • 羌胡 - May be used derogatorily to refer to the Qiang as 'barbarians.'
  • 赞普 - A title used for the kings of Tibet (Tibetan: btsan-po), indicating a Tibetan ruler.
  • 五花营 - Likely refers to a military encampment or formation, possibly a specific term used in the military context of the time.
  • 周公 - A legendary figure from the Zhou dynasty, known for his wisdom and virtue; reference emphasizes moral and historical significance.
  • 德政碑 - A classical term for a stone monument erected in honor of a person’s virtuous governance or public service.

Proper Nouns

  • 羌酋 - Qiang chieftain; consistent with historical context.
  • 赞普 - Zhangpu (Tibetan king); a title, not a personal name.
  • 韦夫人 - Lady Wei; a woman’s name; use 'Lady Wei' for consistency.
  • 钟陵 - Zhongling; a place name; keep as is.
  • 周公 - Duke of Zhou (Zhou Gong); a legendary figure; use full title for clarity.
  • 邠泾 - Binjing; place names; keep as is.
  • 宥州 - Youzhou; a place name; keep as is.
  • 貂蝉 - Diao Chan; a historical figure, but may be a misprint or reference to a different person.

[4052]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有心请白其百姓 - Elliptical phrase meaning 'with a sincere heart, inform the common people.'
  • 讽其州尊与立德政碑 - Meaning 'to advise the governor to erect a memorial stone for virtuous governance.'
  • 长揖而退 - Refers to a formal bow and then departing, indicating respect and politeness.
  • 就五原以语百姓及刺史 - Means 'to go to Wuyuan and speak to the common people and the governor.'
  • 俱以为妖 - Means 'both considered it a strange or supernatural occurrence.'
  • 至沙中又逢昔日神人 - Means 'when he reached the desert, he encountered the same divine person again.'
  • 谢合曰 - Means 'the divine person thanked him and said.'
  • 君为言五原无知之俗 - Means 'you spoke on behalf of the ignorant customs of Wuyuan.'
  • 刺史不明此城当有火灾 - Means 'the governor did not understand that the city was destined to have a fire.'
  • 方与祈求幽府 - Means 'we were about to pray to the underworld.'
  • 吾言于五原之事不谐 - Means 'my words about Wuyuan did not align.'
  • 此意亦息其祸 - Means 'this intention also ceased the disaster.'
  • 不三旬而及矣 - Means 'within three weeks, it came to pass.'
  • 挈女骸骨至奉天 - Means 'he carried the remains of his daughter to Fengtian.'
  • 访得小李村而葬之 - Means 'he found the village of Xiaoli and buried her there.'
  • 明日道侧合遇昔日之女子来谢而言 - Means 'the next day, on the side of the road, he met the same girl who came to thank him and said.'
  • 感君之义 - Means 'grateful for your righteousness.'
  • 吾大父乃贞元中得道之士 - Means 'my grandfather was a sage who attained enlightenment during the Zhenyuan era.'
  • 有演参同契续混元经 - Means 'he had compiled and continued the *Zhenyuan Jing*.'
  • 子能穷之龙虎之丹不日而成矣 - Means 'if you can fully understand the alchemical formula for the Dragon and Tiger elixir, it will soon be complete.'
  • 合受之女子已没 - Means 'he accepted the formula, and the girl had already disappeared.'
  • 居于少室烧之一年皆使瓦砾为金宝 - Means 'he lived in the Shaoshi Mountains and burned for a year, turning bricks and stones into gold and jewels.'
  • 二年能起毙者 - Means 'in two years, he could revive the dead.'
  • 三年饵之能度世 - Means 'in three years, he could use the elixir to transcend the world.'
  • 今时有人遇之于嵩岭耳 - Means 'now, sometimes people encounter him in the Song Mountains.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元 - Refers to the Zhenyuan era (785–805 AD) of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 参同契 - An alchemical text attributed to Wei Boyang.
  • 混元经 - Another alchemical text.
  • 少室 - Refers to the Shaoshi Mountains, a famous Taoist site.
  • 嵩岭 - Refers to the Song Mountains, another significant Taoist location.

Proper Nouns

  • 五原 - The name of a place, possibly a city or region.
  • 参同契 - The title of an alchemical text.
  • 混元经 - The title of another alchemical text.
  • 少室 - The name of a mountain range.
  • 嵩岭 - The name of another mountain range.

[4053]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 擬徃少室尋師 - Compound verb meaning 'intend to go to the Shaoshi Mountains to seek a teacher.'
  • 同入少室師李潛 - Means 'together enter the Shaoshi Mountains to study under Li Qian.'
  • 從今去五載方成名 - Compound phrase meaning 'from now on, five years will pass before one achieves fame.'
  • 官亦不過縣佐 - Means 'one's official position will not exceed that of a county assistant.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河陽 - Refers to Heyang, a place name in Henan Province.
  • 陽翟 - Refers to Yangdi, an ancient city in Henan Province.
  • 少室山 - Refers to the Shaoshi Mountains, a sacred mountain in Henan Province.
  • 李潛 - A historical figure, likely a hermit or sage living in the Shaoshi Mountains.
  • 防司主典 - Ambiguous term; could refer to a guardian or a divine being. '防司' might mean 'guardian' or 'protector,' and '主典' could mean 'chief recorder' or 'principal official.'
  • 泰岳 - Refers to Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains in China, known for its religious and cultural significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 韦安之 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 张道 - The name of the person who provides the prophecy.
  • 李潜 - The name of the hermit or sage.
  • 防司主典 - A term that is ambiguous but could be rendered as 'guardian' or 'principal official.'
  • 泰岳 - The name of Mount Tai.

[4054]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 主者 - A term used to refer to a superior or a person in authority, often translated as 'the master' or 'the one in charge.'
  • 重用 - Means 'to heavily employ' or 'to appoint to a high position.'
  • 才識尚寡 - Literally 'talent and knowledge are still lacking,' implying that the person is not yet fully qualified.
  • 給一年假 - Means 'to grant a one-year leave,' often used metaphorically for a period of training or preparation.
  • 辭君去 - Means 'to bid farewell and depart,' often used in the context of leaving a mentor or teacher.
  • 慎勿泄於人 - Means 'be careful not to reveal to others,' emphasizing secrecy.

  • 归而叟之坐后纬萧障下 - Describes the scene where the elderly man is sitting behind a screen of reed curtains. '归而叟之' means 'returned to the old man,' and '纬萧障下' refers to the reed curtain screen.
  • 时闻稚儿啼号甚痛 - Means 'often heard the young child crying and wailing with great pain.'
  • 每发声叟即曰儿可止事已如此悲哭奈何 - The old man says, 'Child, you can stop. It is already like this; crying will not change anything.'
  • 俄则复啼叟辄以前语解之 - The child cries again, and the old man repeats his previous words to console her.
  • 佐文不谕从而诘之叟则低回他说 - The narrator (佐文) does not understand and asks further, but the old man changes the subject.
  • 佐文因曰孩幼苦寒何不携之近火如此数四叟则携致就炉乃八九岁村女子耳见客初无羞骇但以物画灰若抱沈恨忽而怨咽惊号叟则又以前语解之 - The narrator asks why the child is not brought closer to the fire, and the old man brings her to the fire. The child is an eight or nine-year-old village girl who initially shows no fear but draws patterns in the ashes, seemingly carrying deep resentment, and then suddenly cries out in sorrow. The old man consoles her again.
  • 佐文问之终不得其情须臾平晓叟即遥指东南乔木曰彼袁庄也去此十里而近佐文上马四顾乃穷荒大野曾无人迹独田叟一室耳行三数里逢村妇携酒一壶纸钱副焉见佐文曰此是巨泽道无人客凌晨何自来也佐文具白其事妇乃抚膺长号曰孰谓人之殊途耶佐文细询之其妇曰若客云去夜所寄宿之室则我亡夫之殡闾耳我佣居袁庄七年矣前春夫疾而卒翌日始齓之女又亡贫穷无力父子同瘗焉守志嫠居官不免税孤穷无托遂意再行今夕将适他门故来夫女之瘗告诀耳佐文则与同徃比至昨暮之室乃殡宫也歴歴踪由分明可复妇乃号恸涙如绠縻因弃生业剪发于临湍佛寺役力誓死焉其妇姓王开成四年客有见者 - The narrator rides to the southeast, where he meets a village woman who explains that the place where the guest stayed is her late husband's burial site. She had been working in the village for seven years, and after her husband died, her daughter also passed away. Unable to afford separate burials, they were buried together. She now intends to remarry and comes to bid farewell to her husband and daughter. The narrator accompanies her to the burial site, where she weeps bitterly and decides to renounce her worldly life and enter a temple to dedicate herself to labor and vows of death.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 制作靡得而达矣 - This opening phrase is somewhat elliptical, suggesting a sense of unfulfillment or futility in creation or achievement.
  • 皇王帝霸之道兴亡理乱之体其可闻乎 - This clause is a rhetorical question, asking whether the ways of ruling and the principles of rise and fall can be known or understood.
  • 赞今之小巧而隳张古之大体 - This clause contrasts the praise of trivial matters with the undermining of traditional values, creating a tension between the present and the past.
  • 珠露既清桂月如昼吟咏时发杯觞间行 - This long sequence describes a serene and poetic scene, but the syntax is somewhat compressed, requiring careful unpacking in English.
  • 彼佳人兮如琼之瑛此良马兮负骏之名 - This parallel structure compares the beauty of a woman to the virtue of a horse, but the syntax is somewhat elliptical, requiring careful rendering in English.
  • 将有求于逐日故何惜于倾城 - This clause explains the motivation for sacrificing beauty, but the syntax is somewhat compressed, requiring careful unpacking in English.
  • 希逸曰原夫人以矜其容马乃称其德既各从其所好 - This clause introduces a speaker and their statement, but the syntax is somewhat elliptical, requiring careful rendering in English.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The question '是何篇什得先生赏叹之若是' must be read as a direct address to the scholar, asking why he praises the poem so much.
  • The clause '此诗有似为席中一二公有其题而晦其姓名怜其终章皆有意思乃曰' must be read as an explanation of the poem's content and the scholar's reaction to it.

  • The clause '神貌者云我知作诗人矣得非伊水之上受我推食脱衣之士乎' must be read as the mysterious person identifying the poet.
  • The sequence '久之白衣叟命飞杯行酒数廻而座中欷歔未已白衣叟曰再经旧游' must be read as the elderly man's reaction to the poem and the group's revisiting of old haunts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '王绍夜深读书有人隔窓借笔绍借之于窓上题诗曰' is a long, elliptical construction that must be read as a single event: Wang Shao was studying late at night when someone asked to borrow a pen through the window, and Wang Shao lent it to them, who then wrote a poem on the window.
  • The poem '何人窓下读书声南斗阑干北斗横千里思家归不得春风肠断石头城' describes a scene of someone reading by the window, the arrangement of the stars, and a deep longing for home.
  • The clause '诗讫寂然无声乃知非人也' concludes the event, indicating that after the poem was finished, there was complete silence, and Wang Shao realized that the entity was not human.

  • 天人 - Celestial beings, often associated with deities or supernatural entities in Chinese mythology.

  • 罪业 - Sins or karma, a concept from Buddhism and Daoism.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 去人居止甚近 - Means 'the tower is very close to where people live,' emphasizing proximity.
  • 女常下视其物在空中不能化形 - Means 'the woman often looked down and saw the object in the air unable to change its form,' describing an observation.
  • 至地方与人杂 - Means 'when it reached the ground, it mixed with people,' indicating interaction.
  • 或有白衣尘中者 - Means 'sometimes there were white-clad figures in the dust,' describing a scene.
  • 其物敛手则避 - Means 'the object would bow its hands and avoid,' showing a gesture of respect.
  • 或见枕其头唾其面者 - Means 'sometimes they saw the object resting its head or spitting on faces,' describing actions.
  • 行人悉若不见 - Means 'passersby all acted as if they didn't see it,' indicating obliviousness.
  • 及归女问之 - Means 'when she returned home and asked about it,' setting up a dialogue.
  • 向者君街中有敬之者有戏狎之者何也 - Means 'what happened earlier, did some people in the street respect it and others play with it?' asking for clarification.
  • 物笑曰世有吃牛肉者予得而欺矣 - Means 'the object laughed and said, 'In this world, there are those who eat beef, and I can deceive them,' explaining its behavior.
  • 又经年忽悲泣语女縁已尽 - Means 'after another year, it suddenly wept and told the woman that its fate was over,' indicating a change in mood.
  • 因授一青石大如鸡卵言至家可磨此服之能下毒气 - Means 'it then gave a green stone as big as a chicken's egg and said that when you get home, you can grind it and take it to expel poison,' providing instructions.
  • 后一夕风雨其物遽持女曰可去矣 - Means 'later, on a rainy night, the object quickly took the woman and said, 'You can go now,' indicating a departure.
  • 如释氏言屈伸臂项已至其家坠在庭中其母因磨石饮之下物如青泥斗余 - Means 'as the Buddhist saying goes, stretching an arm and a neck, it had already arrived at her home and fell in the courtyard. Her mother then ground the stone and drank it, and the substance turned into green mud, more than a dou,' describing the outcome.

  • 戒律法箓 - Refers to the precepts and talismans of Buddhism and Taoism, important elements in their practices.
  • 释氏 - A term for Buddhists, often used in classical Chinese texts.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 义也赤发蓬然两目如电四牙若锋刃之状甚可惧 - Vivid description of a fierce, almost supernatural appearance, with '赤发蓬然' (red hair standing on end) and '四牙若锋刃之状' (four fangs like blades).
  • 遽仆于地防然不能动 - '遽仆于地' means 'suddenly fell to the ground,' and '防然不能动' suggests a state of being paralyzed or unable to move.
  • 越石有胆勇即起而逐之 - '有胆勇' means 'courageous,' and '即起而逐之' means 'immediately got up and chased it.'
  • 夜义遂走不敢囘视 - '遂走' means 'ran away,' and '不敢囘视' means 'dared not look back.'
  • 明日穷其迹于垣上有过踪 - '穷其迹' means 'traced its tracks,' and '垣上有过踪' means 'found traces on the wall.'
  • 此物今夕将再来矣 - A prediction that the creature will return that night.
  • 仅食顷夜义果来既逾墙足未及地越石即以杖连撃数十 - '仅食顷' means 'a short while later,' '果来' means 'indeed came,' and '连撃数十' means 'struck it dozens of times.'
  • 夜义去以烛视其垣下血甚多有皮尺余亦在地葢撃而堕者 - '夜义去' means 'the creature left,' '血甚多' means 'there was a lot of blood,' and '有皮尺余亦在地' means 'a piece of skin about a foot long was also on the ground.'
  • 自是张氏病愈至夕闻数里外有呼者曰陈黄石何为不归我皮也连呼不止仅月余毎夕尝闻呼声 - '自是张氏病愈' means 'since then, the Zhang family's illness was cured,' and '连呼不止' means 'kept calling without stopping.'
  • 越石度不可禁且恶其见呼于是迁居以避之因改名越石 - '度不可禁' means 'realized it could not be stopped,' and '迁居以避之' means 'moved to a new home to avoid it.'
  • 元和十五年登第进士至防昌二年卒于蓝田令 - '元和十五年' is the year 820 AD, '登第进士' means 'passed the imperial examination as a Jinshi,' and '至防昌二年卒于蓝田令' means 'died in the second year of the Fanchang era while serving as the magistrate of Lantian.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 越石 - The protagonist, who later changes his name to avoid the creature.
  • 元和十五年 - The year 820 AD, during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 防昌二年 - The second year of the Fanchang era, which is 885 AD.
  • 蓝田令 - Refers to the position of magistrate in Lantian County, Shaanxi Province.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 没李下马就之 - Compound phrase meaning 'Ri dismounted and approached.' '没' here likely means 'to approach' or 'to go to,' though it can also mean 'to dive into' or 'to enter.'
  • 真其妻也 - Means 'it was indeed his wife.' '真' emphasizes the confirmation of identity.
  • 相见悲泣 - Means 'they met and wept.' '相见' means 'to meet,' and '悲泣' means 'to weep with sorrow.'
  • 俟君之来亦已乆矣 - Means 'awaiting your arrival has been for a long time.' '俟' means 'to await,' '君' means 'you,' '之来' means 'your arrival,' and '亦已乆矣' means 'has been for a long time.'
  • 大人刚正不信鬼神 - Means 'my husband is upright and does not believe in ghosts or spirits.' '大人' refers to the husband, '刚正' means 'upright and just,' and '不信鬼神' means 'does not believe in ghosts or spirits.'
  • 身是妇人不能自诉 - Means 'as a woman, she cannot speak for herself.' '身' means 'as a person' or 'as herself,' '是' means 'is,' '妇人' means 'woman,' and '不能自诉' means 'cannot speak for herself.'
  • 今日相见事机较迟 - Means 'meeting today is somewhat late.' '事机' means 'the matter' or 'the situation,' and '较迟' means 'somewhat late.'
  • 为之奈何 - Common expression meaning 'what should be done?' or 'what can be done?'
  • 从此直西五里鄱亭村 - Means 'go straight west for five li to the village of Poting.' '从此' means 'from here,' '直西' means 'straight west,' '五里' means 'five li' (a unit of distance), and '鄱亭村' means 'the village of Poting.'
  • 有一老人姓田方教授村儿 - Means 'there is an old man named Tian who teaches the village children.' '有一老人' means 'there is an old man,' '姓田' means 'named Tian,' '方教授' means 'who is teaching,' and '村儿' means 'village children.'
  • 九华洞中仙官也 - Means 'he is a divine official in the Nine Flower Cave.' '九华洞' means 'Nine Flower Cave,' and '仙官' means 'divine official.'
  • 人莫之知 - Means 'no one knows him.' '人' means 'people,' '莫' means 'no one,' and '之知' means 'knows him.'
  • 君能至心往求或冀谐遂 - Means 'if you sincerely go to seek him, perhaps it will succeed.' '君' means 'you,' '能' means 'can,' '至心' means 'sincerely,' '往求' means 'go to seek,' '或冀' means 'perhaps hope,' and '谐遂' means 'succeed.'
  • 田先生见之乃膝行而前再拜称曰下界凡贱敢谒大仙 - Means 'Mr. Tian saw him and knelt forward, bowing twice, and said, 'A humble mortal from the lower realms dares to pay homage to the great immortal.' '见之' means 'saw him,' '乃' means 'then,' '膝行而前' means 'kneeled forward,' '再拜' means 'bowed twice,' '称曰' means 'said,' '下界凡贱' means 'a humble mortal from the lower realms,' '敢谒' means 'dare to pay homage,' and '大仙' means 'great immortal.'
  • 衰朽穷骨旦暮溘然 - Means 'an old, weak, and soon-to-die body.' '衰朽' means 'old and weak,' '穷骨' means 'poor bones,' '旦暮' means 'morning and evening' (implying soon), and '溘然' means 'suddenly.'
  • 郎君安有此说 - Means 'how can you say such a thing?' '郎君' means 'young master' or 'gentleman,' and '安有此说' means 'how can you say such a thing?'
  • 再拜扣头不已 - Means 'knelt and bowed repeatedly without stopping.' '再拜' means 'knelt and bowed twice,' '扣头' means 'knelt and bowed,' and '不已' means 'without stopping.'
  • 老人益难之 - Means 'the old man found it increasingly difficult.' '老人' means 'the old man,' '益' means 'increasingly,' and '难之' means 'found it difficult.'
  • 自日宴至于夜分终不敢就坐拱立于前 - Means 'from afternoon until midnight, he finally dared not sit down but stood respectfully in front.' '自日宴' means 'from afternoon,' '至于夜分' means 'until midnight,' '终不敢就坐' means 'finally dared not sit down,' and '拱立于前' means 'stood respectfully in front.'
  • 足下诚恳如是吾亦何所焉 - Means 'if you are so sincere, what can I do?' '足下' is a respectful term for 'you,' '诚恳' means 'sincere,' '如是' means 'like this,' '吾亦何所焉' means 'what can I do?'
  • 顿首流涕具云妻枉状 - Means 'knelt and wept, explaining in detail the wrong done to his wife.' '顿首' means 'knelt and bowed,' '流涕' means 'wept,' and '具云妻枉状' means 'explained in detail the wrong done to his wife.'

  • 有一人对曰是西汉长沙王吴 - Formal response: 'one person replies, 'It is the Western Han King of Changsha, Wu.''

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三魂七魄 - Traditional concept in Chinese folklore referring to the soul and spirit, with '三魂' (three souls) and '七魄' (seven spirits) representing different aspects of the human spirit.
  • 死则散离 - Literally means 'die and disperse,' indicating the dispersal of the soul and spirit upon death.
  • 本无所依 - Means 'originally without support,' suggesting a state of being without a physical form.
  • 收合为一体 - Means 'gather and combine into one,' referring to the reuniting of the soul and spirit.
  • 续胶涂之 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a ritual or method of reuniting the soul and spirit, though the exact meaning is unclear.
  • 当街发遣 - Means 'send off in the street,' likely referring to a public ritual or ceremony.
  • 放囘 - Means 'release back,' indicating the return of the soul and spirit to the body.
  • 与本身同矣 - Means 'become the same as the original,' indicating the restoration of the soul and spirit to their original state.
  • 作此处置可乎 - Means 'Is this arrangement acceptable?' or 'Can this be done?'
  • 幸甚 - An expression of great relief or happiness, meaning 'thank goodness' or 'I am very relieved.'
  • 俄见一吏别领七八女人来 - Means 'Soon, a bureaucrat led several women over,' indicating the arrival of a group of women.
  • 推而合之 - Means 'push them together and merge them,' referring to the merging of the soul and spirit.
  • 持一器药状似稀饧 - Means 'holding a vessel containing a substance resembling thin porridge,' possibly referring to a ritual substance or medicine.
  • 于李妻身涂之 - Means 'applied it to Li's wife's body,' indicating the application of the substance to the body.
  • 空中坠地 - Means 'fell from the air to the ground,' describing the sensation of the soul and spirit returning to the body.
  • 初甚迷闷 - Means 'at first very confused and dazed,' describing the initial state of confusion after the ritual.
  • 天明尽失夜来所见 - Means 'by morning, all the night's visions were forgotten,' indicating the fading of the visions or experiences.
  • 唯田先生及李氏夫妻三人共在桑林中 - Means 'only Mr. Tian and the Li couple were left in the mulberry grove,' describing the remaining participants.
  • 相为极力 - Means 'we have done our best for you,' indicating the efforts made on behalf of the person.
  • 且喜事成 - Means 'and now the matter is successful,' indicating the completion of the ritual.
  • 便可领归见其亲族 - Means 'you can take them back to see their relatives,' indicating the next step after the ritual.
  • 但言再生 - Means 'just say they have been reborn,' indicating the explanation to be given to the relatives.
  • 慎无他说 - Means 'be careful not to say anything else,' indicating the need for discretion.
  • 吾亦从此逝矣 - Means 'I will also depart from here,' indicating the end of the ritual and the departure of the practitioner.
  • 自尔生子数人 - Means 'from then on, they had several children,' indicating the continuation of the family.
  • 亲表之中颇有知者 - Means 'among relatives and acquaintances, some knew about it,' indicating that the event was known to some people.
  • 他无所异 - Means 'he is no different in other aspects,' indicating that the person is normal in other ways.
  • 但举止轻便异于常人耳 - Means 'but his movements are light and agile, unlike ordinary people,' indicating a subtle difference in behavior.

The child replied that his grandson's foot was swollen and he couldn't wear the embroidered shoes.

Siu agreed to her request. The two girls also gave her a piece of silk painted with a couple standing together. The child (思儿) wore green and held a small boat, bowing farewell as he left. After he left, the two girls seemed to be in a trance. Two years later, the older daughter married her uncle's son and died in the evening at the tent door, with her form resembling a yellow leaf. The younger daughter married Zhang Chou, and upon her initial marriage, she too seemed to be in a trance. Siu eventually became the magistrate of Shanyang Prefecture.   • 常畏二侄呼为二郎二郎至即不多来 - The woman often fears her two nephews, who are called 'Erlang,' and they rarely come.
  • 李氏潜欲徙其居而得一书曰 - The family of Li attempts to move their residence and receives a letter.
  • 闻君欲徙居吾已先至其所矣 - The letter states that the writer has already arrived at the new residence.
  • 李氏有二老犬一名韩儿一名猛子 - The Li family has two old dogs named Han'er and Mengzi.
  • 自有此妖不复食常摇尾戏于空暗处遂毙之 - The dogs, influenced by the supernatural, stop eating and die.
  • 自后家中有窃议事魅莫能知之 - After this, the family experiences secret matters and supernatural phenomena that are hard to understand.
  • 一书自无韩大 - A letter mentions the absence of 'Han Da.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李氏 - Refers to the Li family, likely a prominent or influential family in the context.
  • 谚所谓一鸡死一鸡鸣 - A common Chinese proverb indicating a cycle of events or retribution.
  • 吾属百户当相报耳 - Suggests a group of a hundred people, possibly a clan or community, taking revenge.
  • 旬时 - A period of ten days, indicating a specific timeframe.
  • 士儒 - Refers to scholars or literati, indicating a learned or intellectual class.
  • 二郎 - A common nickname for a younger brother, possibly indicating a specific person or role.
  • 韩大 - Likely a person's name, possibly a significant figure in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 李氏 - The Li family.
  • 二郎 - A nickname, possibly for a younger brother or a specific person.
  • 韩大 - A person's name, possibly significant in the story.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 猛二吾属无依 - Colloquial expression meaning 'Meng Er and we have no place to rely on.'
  • 家人自郭返至其里见二丈夫于道侧迎问家人曰 - Complex sequence of actions: 'the family returned from the outskirts to their village and saw two men by the roadside who greeted and asked the family.'
  • 闻尔家有怪异若之何遂以事答 - Question followed by an answer: 'heard that your family has some strange occurrences, what should we do? then answered with the situation.'
  • 顾已不见 - Short and clear: 'looked back and they were already gone.'
  • 士昌以符置诸瓦櫺间以压之 - Description of an action: 'Wei Shichang placed talismans on the eaves to suppress them.'
  • 书曰符至圣也而置之屋上不亦轻乎 - Quote from a ghost: 'the talisman is sacred, but placing it on the roof is not a sign of respect.'
  • 士昌无能为乃去 - Statement of failure: 'Wei Shichang could do nothing and left.'
  • 闻淮楚有卫生者久于呪术乃邀之 - Sequence of actions: 'heard that in Huai and Chu there is a man named Wei Sheng who has been practicing magic for a long time, then invited him.'
  • 卫生乃设道塲以考召 - Description of an action: 'Wei Sheng then set up a Daoist ritual to summon them.'
  • 置箱于坛中 - Description of an action: 'placed a box in the altar.'
  • 宿昔箱中得一状 - Statement of discovery: 'the next day, a document was found in the box.'
  • 状件所失物云若干物已货记得钱若干买果子及梳子等 - Detailed list of items and actions: 'the document listed the lost items, stating that some items had been sold, and the money earned was used to buy fruits and combs, etc.'
  • 食讫其余若干并送还 - Statement of return: 'after eating, the rest was returned.'
  • 验其物悉在箱中 - Statement of verification: 'upon checking, all the items were indeed in the box.'
  • 又言失铛子某实不取 - Statement of denial: 'the document also mentioned the loss of a pot, stating that someone did not take it.'
  • 请问诸水濵 - Statement of inquiry: 'asked about it by the water's edge.'
  • 状言狐等状 - Statement of cause: 'the document said it was caused by foxes and other spirits.'
  • 自此更不复来 - Statement of cessation: 'from then on, they did not come back.'
  • 异日于河中果得铛子等 - Statement of discovery: 'later, the pot was indeed found in the river.'
  • 乃验水濵之说也 - Statement of verification: 'thus, the water's edge statement was verified.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 猛二 - A person's name, likely a commoner or local figure.
  • 吾属 - Refers to 'we' or 'us,' indicating a group of people.

  • 韦士昌 - A person's name, likely a Daoist practitioner or magician.

  • 瓦櫺 - Refers to the eaves or roof beams of a house.
  • 卫生 - A person's name, likely a magician or Daoist practitioner.

[4241]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贞元 - Era name of Emperor Dezong of Tang (785–805 CE).
  • 家于 - Classical construction meaning 'resided in.'
  • 正病卒 - Compound term: '正' (the father), '病卒' (died of illness).
  • 正昼 - 'Noon' or 'midday.'
  • 巡翔空间 - Poetic expression for 'flying around in the air' or 'circling the space.'
  • 度其影 - 'Judged by its shadow.'
  • 可濶丈四五 - 'Could be about fifteen to sixteen feet wide.' '濶' is an archaic form of '阔' (wide).
  • 血流数斗 - 'Blood flowed several dou (ancient measure, approx. 3–4 liters).' This is likely a metaphorical or supernatural event, not literal.
  • 至明 - 'Until dawn' or 'until daylight.'
  • 堂西奥 - 'In the west of the hall' or 'in the western part of the hall.' '奥' = 'inner recess' or 'inner part.'
  • 诞子井中 - 'Was born in the well.'
  • 何敢取杀 - 'How dare I take and kill?'
  • 拽其尸如糜散 - 'Dragged her body like it was turning to pulp or dissolving.'
  • 讫奋臂而去 - 'After finishing, she raised her arms and departed.'
  • 出门而灭其家 - 'Exited the door and vanished.'
  • 其家大震惧 - 'The family was greatly terrified.'
  • 取所留卵却送于野 - 'Took the eggs left behind and returned them to the wild.'
  • 使人驰问桑道茂 - 'Sent someone to inquire of Sang Daomao.'
  • 禳谢 - 'To perform rituals of appeasement and thanksgiving.'
  • 征祥 - 'Omens or signs.'
  • 莫测其异 - 'Could not understand the mystery.'
  • 【出通幽记】 - Source citation. '通幽记' is a classical Chinese text, likely *Tongyou Ji* (Record of Connecting with the Other World), a collection of strange tales and supernatural events from the Tang dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元九年 - 793 CE. The text states 'before'贞元九年, so the event took place before 793 CE.
  • 毫州刺史 - '刺史' was a local official in the Tang dynasty, responsible for governance and military affairs in a prefecture. '毫州' (Haozhou) is a historical prefecture in modern-day Anhui province.
  • 东都 - In the Tang dynasty, '东都' (Dongdu) referred to Luoyang, the eastern capital, which was an important political and cultural center.
  • 康裕坊 - A '坊' (fang) was a district or ward in the city of Luoyang, similar to a modern neighborhood or suburb.
  • 桑道茂 - A historical figure, likely a Daoist or mystic, known for his prophetic abilities. He was active during the Tang dynasty and was a court advisor.
  • 通幽记 - A classical Chinese text of supernatural tales, possibly compiled by a Tang dynasty author. It is similar to *In Search of the Supernatural* by Gan Bao, but less well-known.

Proper Nouns

  • 卢瑗 - A person, likely a Tang dynasty official.
  • 桑道茂 - A historical figure, possibly a Daoist or mystic.
  • 通幽记 - Title of the source text.
  • 毫州 - A prefecture in modern-day Anhui.
  • 东都 - Luoyang, the eastern capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 康裕坊 - A district in Luoyang.
  • 刺史 - Prefect or military governor.
  • 禳谢 - Ritual of appeasement and thanksgiving, often used in Daoist or folk religious practices.
  • 征祥 - Omens or signs.

[4242]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 采樵 - To gather firewood — a common activity in ancient China.
  • 山防 - Likely a typo or variant of '山旁' (shān páng), meaning 'beside the mountain' or 'near the mountain.'
  • 伫望 - To stand and look, gazing intently.
  • 状人 - 'In the shape of a person' or 'resembling a human being.'
  • 举体黄毛 - 'All over the body covered in yellow hair.'
  • 数寸 - 'A few inches' — likely describing the length of the hair.
  • 防乌巾 - Possibly a mischaracterization of '方乌巾' (fāng wū jīn), meaning 'square black headscarf' or 'black square cloth on the head.'
  • 遽走匿古木中 - 'Ran quickly and hid among the ancient trees.'
  • 不觉懔然 - 'Unconsciously felt a sense of fear or awe.'
  • 前驱者 - 'Those in the vanguard' or 'the lead figures.'
  • 射其喉 - 'Shoot at its throat.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡人 - A term for 'barbarian' or 'non-Han Chinese people,' often used in classical Chinese to refer to people of Central Asian or northern ethnic groups.
  • 将军 - A title for a general or military commander. In this context, it may be used as an honorific or a title for the largest or most powerful figure among the trappers.
  • The narrative structure resembles zhiguai (志怪) literature, which includes supernatural or strange occurrences, often involving encounters with non-human or semi-human beings.

Proper Nouns

  • 庐江 - A historical county in Anhui province, now part of modern-day China. Should be translated as 'Lüjiang' or 'Lujiang.'
  • 贞元 - A reign title of Emperor Dezong of the Tang dynasty (785–805 CE). Should be translated as 'the Zhēnyuán period.'

[4243]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 非雄舒莫可 - Classical construction meaning 'Only Xiongshu can do it' or 'Only Xiongshu is fit for this.'
  • 徐以手防去三矢 - Slowly with his hand he removed three arrows; 防 may be an archaic form of 防, meaning 'to block' or 'to remove.'
  • 自去其巾状如妇人无发 - Removed his headscarf, appearing like a woman with no hair; suggests transformation or disguise.
  • 连掌其颊 - Clapped his palms on his cheeks; a classical expression of humiliation or punishment.
  • 赖今日甲子耳 - Thanks to today being the day of Jiazi; Jiazi (甲子) is a term from the Chinese sexagenary cycle, often used in divination or as a reference to a significant or fateful day.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡人 - Hu people or barbarians; refers to non-Han ethnic groups, often associated with the northern frontier in Chinese historical narratives.
  • 甲子 - Jiazi; term from the Chinese sexagenary cycle (干支), used in divination, astrology, and timekeeping. Its inclusion may suggest that the day was considered auspicious or fateful.
  • 宣室志 - Xuanshi Zhi; a collection of strange tales and supernatural stories from the Tang dynasty. Similar in genre to Shishuo Xinyu or Zhiwu Zhi, but more focused on the supernatural and the miraculous.

Proper Nouns

  • 雄舒 - Xiongshu; a name, possibly a character in the story. Likely of Chinese origin.
  • 巨胡 - Juhu; 'Great Hu person' or 'Great barbarian,' likely the leader of the group.
  • 宣室志 - Xuanshi Zhi; should be translated as The Records of the Xuanshi or The Xuanshi Zhi.
  • 甲子 - Jiazi; term from the sexagenary cycle; should be translated as 'the Jiazi day' or 'the day of Jiazi.'
  • 巾 - Jin; a headscarf or cloth covering, commonly used in classical Chinese for clothing or disguise.
  • 砾 - Li; a pebble or stone, used here as a weapon or symbol of power.
  • 弓矢 - Gongshi; 'bow and arrows,' a common weapon of the time.
